mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) relative to baseline during rate cuts, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, which compresses corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) when unemployment rises during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal income and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand and construction activity, which reduces aggregate revenue growth and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns (win rate < 45%), or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the industrial production index above its 12-month moving average reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which supports near-term corporate output and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns do not materialize when INDPRO crosses above its moving average during inflationary supply shocks, or Rank IC falls below 0.015 in recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI inflation reading above 3.5% triggers expectations of restrictive monetary policy and margin compression, which reduces equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of high-inflation months, or the signal fails to predict direction during supply-driven inflation episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate household income and consumer demand, which expands corporate revenue projections and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to correlate with positive 60-day returns (Rank IC < 0.02), or positive returns are absent when payroll gains are concentrated in low-wage sectors.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real GDP coinciding with a monthly drop in real personal income signals a broad-based demand shock that immediately erodes earnings forecasts and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines QoQ and RSAFS declines MoM
failure_condition: The joint decline condition produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when GDP declines are driven by inventory adjustments rather than final sales.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production during a low-inflation environment signals healthy economic momentum without policy tightening risks, which supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns do not materialize when industrial production rises alongside CPI inflation above 3.0%, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 50% in high-inflation regimes.
